structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Air Temperature Setpoint Mapping from Heating/Cooling Loop Signals
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: SATsp and SATsp-C setpoints determined from heating/cooling loop signals: heating 100-50% → SATsp = Heat_SAT; 50-0% → SATsp reset from Heat_SAT to deadband; deadband → SATsp = deadband; cooling 0-25% → SATsp resets from deadband to Cool_SAT-1°C, SATsp-C = deadband; 25-50% unchanged; 50-75% SATsp remains at Cool_SAT-1°C, SATsp-C resets from deadband to Cool_SAT; 75-100% unchanged.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Heating or cooling loop signal value
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SATsp and SATsp-C according to signal range as described
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
h. For a heating -loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT.
i. For a heating -loop signal of 50 % to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the deadband value.
j. In deadband, SATsp is the deadband value.
k. For a cooling -loop signal of 0% to 25%, SATsp is reset from the deadband value to Cool_SAT minus 1°C (2°F), while SATsp -C is the deadband value.
l. For a co oling -loop signal of 25% to 50%, SATsp and SATsp -C are unchanged.
m. For a cooling -loop signal of 50% to 75%, SATsp remains at Cool_SAT minus 1°C (2°F), SATsp -C is reset from the deadband value to Cool_SAT.
n. For a cooling -loop signal of 75% to 100%, SATsp and SATsp -C are unchanged.

evidence_quote: 5.18.4.6.  Figure 5.18.4.5 -2 separates Figure 5.18.4.5 -1 in two for clarity and to illustrate the relative
